hypotheses.statement: Improving architecture from 70 to 90+ will increase platform reliability by ≥15%.
hypotheses.rationale: Benchmark baseline shows architecture at 70 — below the 80-point threshold required for production excellence.
hypotheses.measurable: architecture score ≥90 after intervention
hypotheses.falsifiable: If architecture score does not increase by ≥10 points after 2 weeks of changes, the hypothesis is refuted.

hypotheses.statement: Resolving the pattern "Self-healing system only knows one strategy (escalate) and always fails" will reduce engineering failures by ≥20%.
hypotheses.rationale: Pattern observed 2000 times with 95% confidence. Extend SelfHealingRuntime with a strategy ladder: (1) retry if transient, (2) decompose if complex, (3) park-to-DLQ if deterministic, (4) notify-operator if critical. The current single-strategy escalate loop should be the last resort, not the only option.
hypotheses.measurable: Failure rate for this pattern drops to <5% within 30 days
hypotheses.falsifiable: If failure rate does not decrease ≥20% within 30 days, hypothesis is refuted.

hypotheses.statement: Resolving the pattern "Sales agent handler registration race at startup" will reduce engineering failures by ≥20%.
hypotheses.rationale: Pattern observed 1999 times with 88% confidence. Add a readiness gate: autonomous cycles should not dispatch sales tasks until the sales handler emits a 'registered' event. A simple boolean guard in autonomousLoop checked against agentRegistry.isReady('sales') would prevent future recurrence.
hypotheses.measurable: Failure rate for this pattern drops to <5% within 30 days
hypotheses.falsifiable: If failure rate does not decrease ≥20% within 30 days, hypothesis is refuted.

hypotheses.statement: Resolving the pattern "Agent run type "autonomous_cycle_task" has only 0% success rate (249 runs)" will reduce engineering failures by ≥2%.
hypotheses.rationale: Pattern observed 249 times with 100% confidence. Retire or avoid "autonomous_cycle_task" in new missions — consistently failing
hypotheses.measurable: Failure rate for this pattern drops to <5% within 30 days
hypotheses.falsifiable: If failure rate does not decrease ≥20% within 30 days, hypothesis is refuted.

hypotheses.statement: Resolving the pattern ""ako_qa" has generated 197 lessons — rich signal source" will reduce engineering failures by ≥2%.
hypotheses.rationale: Pattern observed 197 times with 75% confidence. Increase evidence weight for "ako_qa" in confidence engine
hypotheses.measurable: Failure rate for this pattern drops to <5% within 30 days
hypotheses.falsifiable: If failure rate does not decrease ≥20% within 30 days, hypothesis is refuted.

hypotheses.statement: Automated health scoring and early warning alerts will reduce customer churn by ≥20%.
hypotheses.rationale: Platform has customer health data but no automated intervention pipeline. Hypothesis tests whether automation closes this gap.
hypotheses.measurable: Churn events decrease ≥20% in the 30 days following automation activation
hypotheses.falsifiable: If churn events do not decrease ≥10% within 30 days, hypothesis is refuted.

hypotheses.statement: Cross-referencing the 513 indexed research findings will surface ≥3 novel algorithm improvements not yet applied to the platform.
hypotheses.rationale: 513 findings are indexed but correlation analysis has not been systematically applied to detect latent algorithm improvements.
hypotheses.measurable: ≥3 algorithm improvement proposals generated and validated within 14 days
hypotheses.falsifiable: If <2 improvement proposals are generated, hypothesis is refuted.

hypotheses.statement: Automated circuit-breaker configuration will reduce infrastructure failure cascades by ≥40%.
hypotheses.rationale: Engineering reasoning shows current circuit-breaker strategy is sub-optimal. Automated tuning via self-healing engine can improve cascade prevention.
hypotheses.measurable: Cascading failure rate drops ≥40% within 21 days of automated tuning
hypotheses.falsifiable: If cascade rate does not drop ≥20%, hypothesis is refuted.
